title: Title for countGLM — countGLM
code: countGLM(   formula,   data,   family = poisson(),   weights = NULL,   subset = NULL,   na.action = getOption("na.action"),   control = glm.control(),   offset = NULL,   ... )

title: Title for countGLM — countGLM
text: formula model formula (e.g. y ~ x1 + x2). data data frame containing variables formula. family description error distribution link function used model (default: gaussian()). weights Optional numeric vector observation weights. subset Optional expression indicating subset rows use. na.action Function indicates happen data contain NAs. control list control parameters (document elements use; e.g. glm.control). offset Optional offset. ... Additional arguments passed lower-level methods.

title: Title for countGLM — countGLM
text: object class countGLM (list containing least: coefficients, fitted.values, residuals, vcov, converged). Describe components precisely.

title: Title for countGLM — countGLM
text: Longer description algorithm, assumptions, important notes (e.g. handling singularities, convergence criteria, warnings produced).

what: Plot count data against predictors — plot_count_data
title: Plot count data against predictors — plot_count_data
text: Internal plotting function called summarizeCountData. Selects appropriate ggplot based number type predictors supplied.

title: Plot count data against predictors — plot_count_data
Plot count data against predictors — plot_count_data
text: y Numeric vector non-negative integer counts (response). mf model frame returned model.frame. pred_vars Character vector predictor variable names plot. two names supplied, first two used warning issued. pred_types Named character vector classifying element pred_vars either "continuous" "categorical".

title: Plot count data against predictors — plot_count_data
Plot count data against predictors — plot_count_data
text: See summarizeCountData full description plot type produced predictor combination.

title: Summarize count data — summarizeCountData
text: Produces numerical graphical summary count response variable, plot automatically adapting number type predictors formula.

title: Summarize count data — summarizeCountData
text: formula formula form y ~ x1 + x2 + ... y non-negative integer count response. Offsets via offset() supported. data data frame containing variables formula.

title: Summarize count data — summarizeCountData
text: named list three elements: summary one-row data.frame containing: mean, var, var_mean_ratio, n_zero, n_total. counts data.frame columns count freq giving frequency observed count value. plot ggplot object. plot type depends number type predictors — see Details.

title: Summarize count data — summarizeCountData
text: graphical summary chosen based predictors formula: predictors Histogram count response. One continuous predictor Scatter plot loess smooth. One categorical predictor Violin plot jittered points. Two continuous predictors 2D bin plot (geom_bin2d) viridis fill scale. Two categorical predictors Tile heatmap mean counts. One continuous, one categorical predictor Scatter plot loess smooths coloured categorical variable. Three predictors warning issued first two predictors used. var_mean_ratio summary table variance--mean ratio. value close 1 consistent Poisson distribution; values substantially greater 1 suggest overdispersion.

title: Summarize count data — summarizeCountData
code: set.seed(1) df <- data.frame(   y  = rpois(100, lambda = 3),   x1 = rnorm(100),   x2 = sample(c("A", "B"), 100, replace = TRUE) )  # No predictors summarizeCountData(y ~ 1, data = df) #> $summary #>   mean     var var_mean_ratio n_zero n_total #> 1 3.05 2.14899      0.7045869      2     100 #>  #> $counts #>   count freq #> 1     0    2 #> 2     1   11 #> 3     2   28 #> 4     3   20 #> 5     4   23 #> 6     5   12 #> 7     6    3 #> 8     8    1 #>  #> $plot  #>   # One continuous predictor summarizeCountData(y ~ x1, data = df) #> $summary #>   mean     var var_mean_ratio n_zero n_total #> 1 3.05 2.14899      0.7045869      2     100 #>  #> $counts #>   count freq #> 1     0    2 #> 2     1   11 #> 3     2   28 #> 4     3   20 #> 5     4   23 #> 6     5   12 #> 7     6    3 #> 8     8    1 #>  #> $plot #> `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'  #>   # One categorical predictor summarizeCountData(y ~ x2, data = df) #> $summary #>   mean     var var_mean_ratio n_zero n_total #> 1 3.05 2.14899      0.7045869      2     100 #>  #> $counts #>   count freq #> 1     0    2 #> 2     1   11 #> 3     2   28 #> 4     3   20 #> 5     4   23 #> 6     5   12 #> 7     6    3 #> 8     8    1 #>  #> $plot  #>   # Mixed predictors summarizeCountData(y ~ x1 + x2, data = df) #> $summary #>   mean     var var_mean_ratio n_zero n_total #> 1 3.05 2.14899      0.7045869      2     100 #>  #> $counts #>   count freq #> 1     0    2 #> 2     1   11 #> 3     2   28 #> 4     3   20 #> 5     4   23 #> 6     5   12 #> 7     6    3 #> 8     8    1 #>  #> $plot #> `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'  #>
